title: About EpiEvidence
text:
About EpiEvidence
EpiEvidence is a personal website and knowledge platform founded by Yangjun Liu, where she shares insights on epidemiology, data science, evidence-based research, and scientific discovery.
The platform aims to make complex research methods, analytical approaches, and emerging evidence more accessible to researchers, healthcare professionals, students, and decision-makers.

title: About EpiEvidence
text:
Mission
To advance health through evidence, empower research with intelligence, and enable better decisions through rigorous science, epidemiology, and data-driven insights.

title: About EpiEvidence
text:
Founder
Yangjun Liu is an MD–PhD-trained epidemiologist and data scientist with a medical degree from Central South University (a top-10 university in Medicine in China) and doctoral and postdoctoral training at Karolinska Institutet (a top-10 university in Medicine worldwide).
Yangjun Liu brings a combination of advanced epidemiological expertise and clinical insight. With more than 13 years of research experience, including over 8 years dedicated to epidemiological studies, she has built a strong track record in randomized controlled trials and real-world evidence research. Her expertise spans patient-reported outcomes, epidemiologic study design, advanced statistical analysis, causal inference, results interpretation, and scientific writing across diverse data sources and disease areas. As a former physician with intensive residency training and a medical license, she also brings a deep understanding of clinical medicine and real-world patient care, enabling her to bridge rigorous research with meaningful clinical impact.
Yangjun Liu is proficient in multiple statistical software, including R, SAS, Stata, and Mplus.

title: About EpiEvidence
text:
Disclaimer
EpiEvidence is a personal website. The views and opinions expressed on this website are solely those of the author and do not necessarily reflect the views of any current or former employer, client, or affiliated organization.
